TOP_PERCENTILE.purpose: Fraction of samples labelled class 1. Top X% = HIGH values = GOOD.
TOP_PERCENTILE.rationale: Top 30% gives 8 positive samples. Balances selectivity vs having enough training signal.

N_CANDIDATES.purpose: Total candidate pool size evaluated by classifier+GP before selecting 1 to submit.
N_CANDIDATES.rationale: 10,000 provides dense coverage in 4D space. Larger = better coverage but slower GP.

EXPLOIT_RATIO.purpose: Fraction of candidates sampled near best-known point (exploitation) vs random (exploration).
EXPLOIT_RATIO.rationale: 85% exploitation justified at Week 7 — strong best point established. Early weeks: 0.6-0.7. Later weeks: 0.8-0.9.

EXPLOIT_SIGMA.purpose: Gaussian std dev for exploitation sampling around best point.
EXPLOIT_SIGMA.rationale: sigma=0.048 in [0,1]^4 creates ±0.10 search radius. Tight enough to stay near best, loose enough to find local improvements.

UCB_KAPPA.purpose: Exploration weight in UCB = mu + kappa*sigma. Higher = more exploratory.
UCB_KAPPA.rationale: kappa=2.0 ~ 95% CI. Standard choice for Week 7. Use 3-5 early in search, 1-2 when exploiting known best region.

EI_XI.purpose: Exploration jitter in EI. EI = (mu - y_best - xi)*Phi(Z) + sigma*phi(Z).
EI_XI.rationale: xi=0.01 prevents EI from over-committing to tiny improvements near best. Small value: slight nudge toward exploration.

GP_RESTARTS.purpose: Random restarts for GP kernel hyperparameter optimisation.
GP_RESTARTS.rationale: 8 restarts balances fit quality vs runtime. Use 10+ for final weeks when GP quality is critical.

FILTER_PERCENTILE.purpose: Discard bottom X% of candidates by classifier P(class=1) before GP.
FILTER_PERCENTILE.rationale: Keeps 5,000 of 10,000 for GP. Removes clearly bad regions efficiently.

NN_ARCHITECTURES.purpose: Three NN sizes tested to match function landscape complexity.
NN_ARCHITECTURES.rationale: Small=simple/linear landscapes. Medium=general purpose. Large=highly non-linear. Early stopping prevents overfitting.

gp_kernel.rationale: Matern 5/2 is robust for BBO: allows sharp transitions, assumes twice-differentiable function. ConstantKernel scales amplitude. normalize_y stabilises fitting when y range is large.
